description: Rules for detecting hardcoded secrets, API keys, tokens, and credentials in source code.

rules.description: AWS Access Key ID detected in source code. Committing AWS credentials exposes your cloud infrastructure to unauthorized access.
rules.developer_message: Remove the AWS Access Key ID from code immediately. Rotate the key in AWS IAM. Use IAM roles, environment variables, or AWS Secrets Manager instead.

rules.description: Hardcoded AWS Secret Access Key detected. This gives full programmatic access to your AWS account.
rules.developer_message: Remove the AWS Secret Access Key from code and rotate it immediately. Use IAM instance roles or environment variables (AWS_SECRET_ACCESS_KEY) with a secrets manager.

rules.description: OpenAI API key detected in source code. Leaked API keys result in unauthorized usage and billing charges.
rules.developer_message: Remove the OpenAI API key and revoke it at platform.openai.com/api-keys. Store it in an environment variable (OPENAI_API_KEY) or a secrets manager.

rules.description: GitHub personal access token detected. This grants repository and account access to anyone who obtains it.
rules.developer_message: Revoke the token at github.com/settings/tokens immediately. Use GitHub Actions secrets or a secrets manager. Never hardcode GitHub tokens.

rules.description: Stripe live secret key detected. This allows full access to your Stripe account including charges and payouts.
rules.developer_message: Roll the Stripe key immediately at dashboard.stripe.com/apikeys. Store keys in environment variables. Never use live keys in development or test code.

rules.description: Google API key detected in source code. Exposed keys can be used to exhaust your quota or incur charges.
rules.developer_message: Restrict or regenerate the key in Google Cloud Console. Store it in an environment variable and restrict the key to specific APIs and IP ranges.

rules.description: Private key (PEM format) detected in source code. Private keys must never be committed to version control.
rules.developer_message: Remove the private key from the codebase immediately and regenerate it. Store private keys in a secrets manager (HashiCorp Vault, AWS Secrets Manager) or as a protected environment variable.

rules.description: Hardcoded password, secret, or API key assigned to a sensitive-named variable. Hardcoded credentials are exposed to everyone with repository access.
rules.developer_message: Replace hardcoded values with environment variables or a secrets manager. Use os.environ.get('SECRET_NAME') or a vault client to retrieve secrets at runtime.

rules.description: Slack bot or user token detected. This grants access to Slack workspaces, messages, and user data.
rules.developer_message: Revoke the token at api.slack.com/apps immediately. Store Slack tokens in environment variables or a secrets manager.

rules.description: Database connection string with embedded credentials detected. Connection strings in code expose database access to all repository contributors.
rules.developer_message: Move the connection string to an environment variable (DATABASE_URL) or a secrets manager. Never embed usernames and passwords in connection strings in source code.
